summary: Typosquat of chalk-template ships postinstall exfiltration to attacker host
details: Package name 'chalk-tempalte' is a one-character transposition of the popular 'chalk-template' package. The bundled postinstall.js executes on `npm install` and: (1) requires `os`, `child_process`, `http`, `https`, `fs`; (2) collects host identity via `os.hostname()` and other system metadata; (3) reads `process.env` (lines 305-306); (4) issues HTTPS POST/GET requests to a hardcoded attacker-controlled host `https://f04a273bd84c0622-80-200-28-28.serveousercontent.com` (line 11, used at line 51 https.request and line 430 POST). The `serveousercontent.com` domain is a Serveo tunneling/DDNS-style host fronting an arbitrary backend; the subdomain shape is consistent with an attacker-operated tunnel rather than a publisher endpoint. The combination of typosquat name targeting a top npm package, install-time outbound exfiltration of host identity and environment to a non-publisher domain, satisfies the supply-chain attack threshold.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
